references.notes: The term 'al-Manāyā' (fates/deaths) is used here to personify an irresistible force of destiny, a common pre-Islamic cosmological concept.
The term 'al-Manāyā' (fates/deaths) is again used to denote a powerful, almost personified force of destiny, aligning with pre-Islamic cosmological views.
This verse explicitly refers to the pre-Islamic practice of using arrows (azlām) for divination to determine fate, specifically in the context of Abd Allah's near-sacrifice.
The phrase 'if what I saw in the dream is true' indicates a belief in dream interpretation as a form of divination or prophetic insight.
This verse explicitly mentions 'idols' (al-aṣnām) as objects of worship in pre-Islamic Arabian religion, which are representations of deities.
